topResolution: Fix 'allprojects/subprojects Blocks Detected (Configuration Anti-Pattern)' to improve score by approx +18pts

issues.title: allprojects/subprojects Blocks Detected (Configuration Anti-Pattern)
issues.reasoning: Root build.gradle.kts uses allprojects{} or subprojects{} blocks which force eager configuration of all modules and are incompatible with Configuration Cache and Isolated Projects.
issues.impact: Forces Gradle to configure every module even when only one is being built. Blocks migration to Configuration Cache and the upcoming Isolated Projects feature in Gradle 9.x.
issues.resolution: Replace with convention plugins applied via plugins{} block in each module, or use a shared build-logic/ included build with precompiled script plugins.
issues.roiAfterFix: Enables Configuration Cache, reduces configuration time by 50-90%, and prepares for Gradle 9.x Isolated Projects.

issues.title: No Test Sources Found (Dark Module)
issues.reasoning: Module 'nowinandroid' has no test files in src/test, src/androidTest, or src/commonTest. This module is completely untested.
issues.impact: Untested modules are invisible risk vectors. Bugs ship silently, regressions go undetected, and refactoring becomes dangerous. In large projects, dark modules become unmaintainable over time.
issues.resolution: Add at least unit tests for public API classes. Create src/test/kotlin/ directory and add basic tests using JUnit5 or KotlinTest. Target >60% line coverage for business logic modules.
issues.roiAfterFix: Early bug detection, safe refactoring, reduced QA cycle time. Each caught regression saves 2-8h of developer time.

issues.title: Version Catalog May Have Unused Entries
issues.reasoning: The version catalog defines 103 library entries. Some may not be used across the project. Run a full-project scan to identify dead entries.
issues.impact: Unused catalog entries add noise and confusion, making it harder for developers to know which libraries are available and actively used.
issues.resolution: Review gradle/libs.versions.toml and remove entries not referenced by any module. Consider using the 'version-catalog-update' plugin for automated cleanup.
issues.roiAfterFix: Cleaner catalog, less confusion during dependency upgrades.

issues.title: Version Catalog Bundle Opportunities (9)
issues.reasoning: Related dependencies could be grouped into bundles for easier usage: android (3 libs), androidx (50 libs), coil (3 libs)
issues.impact: Without bundles, developers must remember to add multiple related dependencies individually, risking incomplete setups.
issues.resolution: Add [bundles] section in libs.versions.toml grouping related libraries. Example: compose = ["compose-ui", "compose-material", "compose-tooling"]
issues.roiAfterFix: Simpler build files, consistent dependency sets across modules.

issues.title: Dependency Locking Not Configured
issues.reasoning: No dependency locking is configured. Without locking, builds may resolve different transitive versions over time, leading to non-reproducible builds.
issues.impact: A transitive dependency could be silently upgraded to a compromised version between builds. Dependency locking ensures byte-for-byte reproducible resolution.
issues.resolution: Enable locking: dependencyLocking { lockAllConfigurations() } and commit lockfiles to VCS. Run ./gradlew dependencies --write-locks to generate initial lockfiles.
issues.roiAfterFix: Reproducible builds, supply chain security, deterministic CI/CD.

issues.title: JDK Toolchain Not Configured
issues.reasoning: No JDK toolchain is configured. Builds may use different JDK versions across developer machines and CI, leading to inconsistent bytecode and potential compatibility issues.
issues.impact: Different JDK versions produce different bytecode optimizations. Without toolchain pinning, a developer on JDK 21 may produce incompatible artifacts for a JDK 17 target.
issues.resolution: Add kotlin { jvmToolchain(17) } or java { toolchain { languageVersion.set(JavaLanguageVersion.of(17)) } } to your build file.
issues.roiAfterFix: Consistent builds across all environments, automatic JDK provisioning.
